title: Hymenachne stagnatilis
taxonomy: Hymenachne stagnatilis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, including Australia, Africa, and Asia. This species of grass is known to be invasive in some areas, outcompeting native vegetation and altering local ecosystems. Hymenachne stagnatilis is also commonly referred to as "water fringe grass" or "mud sedge" due to its ability to thrive in aquatic environments. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to diverse environments.
